title: rosa canina var. sylvularum
intro: Rosa canina var. sylvularum, also known as the wild rose or dog rose, is a deciduous shrub native to Europe and western Asia. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its high content of vitamin C and flavonoids, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Rosa canina var. sylvularum include its potential to support cardiovascular health, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Rosa canina var. sylvularum can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or oil, and is often used to treat colds, sore throats, and digestive issues.
